turns.user_message: I recently caught myself constantly downplaying my own opinions whenever I speak up in a group. It just started happening out of nowhere a few weeks ago, and it feels really strange. I used to just say what I was thinking without overthinking it, but now I immediately backtrack. I keep telling myself it is just a passing phase, yet I notice my voice getting quieter every single time. It makes me feel so small and completely out of place.

This habit has completely taken over my daily interactions, and I cannot seem to shake the heavy feeling of inadequacy. Every time I share a thought, I instantly assume it sounds foolish or completely irrelevant to everyone else. I spend hours replaying simple conversations in my head, picking apart every single word I said. It feels like I am fundamentally broken in a way that nobody else seems to notice. I just wish I could trust my own instincts again instead of constantly doubting my value.

I am completely exhausted from trying to shrink myself down to fit into spaces that were never meant for me. The constant internal criticism has turned into a loud, relentless voice that tells me I am utterly worthless. I catch myself apologizing for simply existing in a room, convinced that my presence is just an inconvenience to everyone around me. It hurts so much to realize how deeply I have convinced myself that I do not deserve to take up any space at all. I just feel completely hollow and entirely convinced that I will never be enough.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This just started recently.
